installation.title: Installation
installation.content:
# Installation

Getting started with Rad UI is simple and quick! Whether you're integrating it into an existing project or starting fresh, you'll be up and running in no time. Follow the instructions below to install Rad UI using your preferred package manager.

## Install via npm

If you use npm, run the following command to install Rad UI:

```bash
npm install @radui/ui --save

javascript.code:
"use client" import type { ComponentProps } from "react" import Accordion from "@radui/ui/Accordion" import { ChevronDown } from "lucide-react" // Mirrors `WithAnimation` in `src/components/ui/Accordion/stories/Accordion.stories.tsx` const items = [ { title: "The Matrix (1999)", content: (
  • Summary: A hacker discovers the true nature of reality and his role in the war against its controllers.
  • Key Characters: Neo, Morpheus, Trinity, Agent Smith
  • Memorable Quote: "There is no spoon."
) }, { title: "The Dark Knight (2008)", content: (
  • Summary: Batman faces his greatest challenge yet as the Joker wreaks havoc on Gotham City.
  • Key Characters: Batman, Joker, Harvey Dent, Alfred
  • Memorable Quote: "Why so serious?"
) }, { title: "Inception (2010)", content: (
  • Summary: A thief who enters people's dreams to steal their secrets must plant an idea in someone's mind.
  • Key Characters: Cobb, Ariadne, Mal, Saito
  • Memorable Quote: "You mustn't be afraid to dream a little bigger, darling."
) }, { title: "The Shawshank Redemption (1994)", content: (
  • Summary: A banker is wrongly convicted
  • Key Characters: Andy Dufresne, Red, Warden Norton, Tommy
  • Memorable Quote: "Get busy living or get busy dying."
) } ] type AccordionExampleProps = ComponentProps const AccordionExample = ({ ...args }: AccordionExampleProps) => { return (
{items.map((item, index) => ( {item.title} {item.content} ))}
) } export default AccordionExample

root.name: Root
root.description: The root component for the Accordion.

prop.name: className
prop.info_tooltips: The class name for the AccordionRoot.

prop.name: asChild
prop.info_tooltips: Whether to use the child component as the Accordion.

prop.name: loop
prop.info_tooltips: Whether to loop through the Accordion items.

prop.name: orientation
prop.info_tooltips: The orientation of the Accordion.

prop.name: disableTabIndexing
prop.info_tooltips: Disables the roving tabindex behavior for keyboard navigation.

prop.name: disabled
prop.info_tooltips: Disables all accordion items.

prop.name: transitionDuration
prop.info_tooltips: Transition duration in milliseconds for item open and close animations.

prop.name: transitionTimingFunction
prop.info_tooltips: CSS timing function used by accordion transitions.

prop.name: openMultiple
prop.info_tooltips: Deprecated alias for allowing multiple items to be open at once. Prefer type='multiple'.

prop.name: type
prop.info_tooltips: Selection mode for the accordion.

prop.name: collapsible
prop.info_tooltips: When type is single, allows the currently open item to be closed.

prop.name: value
prop.info_tooltips: Controlled open item values.

prop.name: defaultValue
prop.info_tooltips: Initial open item values for uncontrolled usage.

prop.name: onValueChange
prop.info_tooltips: Called whenever the set of open item values changes.

item.name: Item
item.description: The item component for the Accordion.

prop.name: className
prop.info_tooltips: The class name for the AccordionItem.

prop.name: asChild
prop.info_tooltips: Whether to use the child component as the AccordionItem.

prop.name: disabled
prop.info_tooltips: Whether to disable the AccordionItem.

prop.name: value*
prop.info_tooltips: The item value used for controlled state. If omitted, an internal id is generated.

header.name: Header
header.description: Wraps an Accordion.Trigger in the appropriate heading semantics.

prop.name: className
prop.info_tooltips: Additional class names for the Accordion.Header wrapper.

prop.name: asChild
prop.info_tooltips: Render the header as the child element instead of a native heading.

trigger.name: Trigger
trigger.description: The trigger component for the Accordion. This component is used to toggle the visibility of the AccordionItem.

prop.name: className
prop.info_tooltips: The class name for the AccordionTrigger.

prop.name: asChild
prop.info_tooltips: Whether to use the child component as the AccordionTrigger.

prop.name: id
prop.info_tooltips: Optional id forwarded to the roving focus item and trigger button.

content.name: Content
content.description: Contains the collapsible panel for an Accordion item.

prop.name: className
prop.info_tooltips: Additional class names for the Accordion.Content element.

prop.name: forceMount
prop.info_tooltips: Keep the content mounted even when the item is closed.

prop.name: asChild
prop.info_tooltips: Render the content as the child element instead of a default div wrapper.

data.description:
<Text>
              When focus is on an Accordion.Trigger of a collapsed section, expands the section.
            </Text>

<Text>
            When focus is on an Accordion.Trigger of an expanded section, collapses the section.
          </Text>

<Text>
            When focus is on an Accordion.Trigger of a collapsed section, focuses the next Accordion.Trigger.
          </Text>

<Text>
            When focus is on an Accordion.Trigger of an expanded section, focuses the previous Accordion.Trigger.
          </Text>

<Text>
            When focus is on an Accordion.Trigger of a collapsed section, focuses the next Accordion.Trigger.
          </Text>

<Text>
            When focus is on an Accordion.Trigger of an expanded section, focuses the previous Accordion.Trigger.
          </Text>

<Text>
            When focus is on an Accordion.Trigger, focuses the first Accordion.Trigger. [TODO]
          </Text>

<Text>
            When focus is on an Accordion.Trigger, focuses the last Accordion.Trigger. [TODO]
          </Text>

anatomy.code:
import AlertDialog from "@radui/ui/AlertDialog";

export default () => {
    return (
        <AlertDialog.Root>
            <AlertDialog.Trigger>Open alert</AlertDialog.Trigger>
            <AlertDialog.Portal>
                <AlertDialog.Overlay/>
                <AlertDialog.Content>
                    <AlertDialog.Title>Alert dialog title</AlertDialog.Title>
                    <AlertDialog.Description>Alert dialog description</AlertDialog.Description>
                    <AlertDialog.Footer>
                        <AlertDialog.Cancel>Cancel</AlertDialog.Cancel>
                        <AlertDialog.Action>Confirm</AlertDialog.Action>
                    </AlertDialog.Footer>
                </AlertDialog.Content>
            </AlertDialog.Portal>
        </AlertDialog.Root>
    )
}

blockquote.name: BlockQuote
blockquote.description: A component used to highlight and distinguish quoted content from the surrounding text, often used for testimonials, citations, or emphasis.

prop.info_tooltips: The content to be displayed inside the blockquote.

prop.name: className
prop.info_tooltips: Additional CSS class names to apply to the blockquote.

prop.name: customRootClass
prop.info_tooltips: Custom root class name to override default styling.

prop.name: variant
prop.info_tooltips: Style variant of the blockquote.

prop.name: size
prop.info_tooltips: Size variant of the blockquote (small, medium, large, x-large).

prop.name: color
prop.info_tooltips: Color theme of the blockquote.

features: Multiple size options: small, medium, large, x-large
Customizable with different color themes
Clean and simple styling for emphasized quotes
Follows typography best practices
Perfect for testimonials and citations

data.description: Accent Color of the quote

prop.name: color
prop.info_tooltips: Color theme of the button.

prop.name: disabled

features: Multiple style variants: solid, soft, outline, ghost
Different size options for various contexts
Customizable with different color themes
Support for icons and text content
Follows accessibility best practices
Can be used as buttons, links, or form submitters

root.name: Root

footer.name: Footer

prop.info_tooltips: Additional CSS class names for the footer wrapper.

action.name: Action

features: Provides a consistent container with proper spacing and borders
Supports custom styling through className and customRootClass
Supports compound subcomponents for structured layouts
Supports visual variant and size tokens
Works well with other components to create structured layouts

anatomy.code:
import Dialog from "@radui/ui/Dialog";

export default () => {
    return (
        <Dialog.Root>
            <Dialog.Trigger>Open dialog</Dialog.Trigger>
            <Dialog.Portal>
                <Dialog.Overlay/>
                <Dialog.Content>
                    <Dialog.Title>Dialog title</Dialog.Title>
                    <Dialog.Description>Dialog description</Dialog.Description>
                    <Dialog.Footer>
                        <Dialog.Close>Close</Dialog.Close>
                    </Dialog.Footer>
                </Dialog.Content>
            </Dialog.Portal>
        </Dialog.Root>
    )
}

em.name: Em
em.description: A component for emphasizing text by rendering it as an <em> element with italic styling.

prop.name: children
prop.info_tooltips: The text content to emphasize.

prop.name: className
prop.info_tooltips: Additional CSS class names to apply to the em element.

prop.name: customRootClass
prop.info_tooltips: Custom root class name to override default styling.

features: Renders as semantic <em> HTML element
Applies italic styling by default
Maintains proper emphasis semantics for accessibility
Customizable with className and style props

heading.name: Heading
heading.description: A component for displaying headings with different levels (h1-h6) and customizable styling.

prop.name: as
prop.info_tooltips: The HTML heading element to render.

prop.name: children
prop.info_tooltips: The content of the heading.

prop.name: className
prop.info_tooltips: Additional CSS class names to apply to the heading element.

prop.name: customRootClass
prop.info_tooltips: Custom root class name to override default styling.

features: Six different heading levels (h1-h6)
Each heading level has appropriate styling out of the box
Preserves semantic meaning with proper HTML tags
Customizable with className and style props

data.description: Allows the component to render different heading levels (h1 to h6)

kbd.name: Kbd
kbd.description: A component that visually represents keyboard buttons or key combinations, often used in documentation to indicate keyboard shortcuts or commands.

anatomy.code:
import Table from "@radui/ui/Table";

export default () => {
    return (
        <Table.Root>
            <Table.Head>
                <Table.Row>
                    <Table.ColumnCellHeader>Column header</Table.ColumnCellHeader>
                </Table.Row>
            </Table.Head>
            <Table.Body>
                <Table.Row>
                    <Table.Cell>Cell</Table.Cell>
                </Table.Row>
            </Table.Body>
        </Table.Root>
    )
}
